docs.title: Baseprints Interoperability Forum
docs.text: This website and associated GitHub repository are for sharing information about Baseprint open formats. The goal is to help users and developers assess the interoperability between applications that read or write in a Baseprint format.

docs.title: Baseprint Open Formats
docs.text: Baseprint Document Format (BDF): This format encodes a Baseprint document snapshot. Document Succession Git Layout (DSGL): Baseprint document snapshots are stored inside a document succession in DSGL format. Document Succession Identifiers (DSI): A DSI is a textual identifier for document successions and snapshots.

docs.title: Baseprint Feature D's
docs.text: The GitHub Discussions category Baseprint Feature D's is available to facilitate discussions, decisions, proposals, definitions, etc. that focus on a specific feature of a Baseprint format.

docs.title: Applications
docs.text: DSI, DSGL, and BDF together make Baseprint document successions, which are introduced on try.perm.pub.

docs.title: Software Reading/Writing in DSGL
docs.text: Hidos: Software library and tool that reads/writes document successions in DSGL. Document Succession Highly Manual Toolkit: Manual for software toolkit that reads/writes document successions in DSGL.

docs.title: Software Writing in a Baseprint Format
docs.text: Baseprinter: A BDF authoring tool that can be used via GitHub Actions, container, or locally installed. Pandoc: This tool can output JATS XML, which can be BDF compatible.

docs.title: Software Reading in a Baseprint Format
docs.text: BaseprintPress: Software generating web pages from Baseprint document successions. For a tutorial, see "Generate Pages for a Published Digital Succession". epijats: An open-source library used by BaseprintPress, perm.pub, popgen.es, and Baseprinter (for previews).

docs.title: Related Technologies
docs.text:
• JATS4R
  • PMC Open Access Subset packages
  • Software Heritage Archive
  • SoftWare Hash Identifier (SWHID)

docs.title: History
docs.text: "Baseprint" was the clear winner in this word choice survey.

docs.title: Baseprint Document Format (BDF)
docs.text:
BDF is a specific format for encoding a Baseprint document snapshot. These snapshots can be referenced with a:

  • SoftWare Hash IDentifier SWHID, and
  • Document Succession Identifier (DSI) plus edition number, as found inside a perm.pub URL.

Baseprint document snapshots exemplify the concept of "baseprint" discussed in the document "What is a baseprint?".

Technically, BDF is not a file format but a format for a directory-like data structure. This structure is addressable as a Git tree and SWHID directory.

When working with BDF data, it is often temporarily stored in a file system directory. However, for public long-term storage, BDF data is stored in a SWHID addressable Git tree or an equivalent "directory" object in the Software Heritage Archive.

Inside BDF, there is a file named article.xml encoded in a subset of the JATS XML format. This file format can informally be referred to as Baseprint JATS XML. As of October 2023, all applications that read Baseprint document snapshots encoded in BDF use the epijats Python library.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text: BDF aims to minimize format rot. Unlike formats like LaTeX and Markdown, which are used for authoring, BDF is designed for archiving.
